name: Rule of 40
summary: A SaaS health metric: your revenue growth rate plus your profit margin should exceed 40%. Balances growth and profitability.
whenToUseIt: When evaluating overall business health, especially for SaaS businesses. Useful when deciding how much to invest in growth versus profitability.
steps: Calculate your annual revenue growth rate (%)
Calculate your profit margin (net income / revenue as %)
Score above 40 = healthy balance of growth and profit
Score below 40 = investigate whether you are growing too slowly or spending too much
example: Company A: 60% growth + -20% margin = 40 (healthy — burning cash for fast growth). Company B: 10% growth + 20% margin = 30 (below 40 — growing too slowly). Company C: 30% growth + 15% margin = 45 (strong — balanced).
